intro: Synostemon arenosus, also known as sandhill synostemon, is a shrub native to Western Australia. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which may aid in wound healing and reduce inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Synostemon arenosus include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Sandhill synostemon is traditionally used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal herb.
